flashcards.front: What is the size of an IPv4 address?
flashcards.back: 32 bits, represented as four octets separated by dots (e.g., 192.168.1.1).

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of subnetting?
flashcards.back: To divide large networks into smaller subnets for efficient IP allocation and reduced broadcast domains.
Classless Inter-Domain Routing notation showing IP address and subnet mask bits (e.g., 192.168.1.0/24).

flashcards.front: How many bits are in an IPv6 address?
flashcards.back: 128 bits, represented as eight groups of four hexadecimal digits.
32-bit number that masks IP address to distinguish network and host portions.

flashcards.front: What are private IP address ranges?
What is the loopback address in IPv4?
flashcards.back: 127.0.0.1, used for testing network software without physical network.
Variable Length Subnet Masking - allows different subnet sizes within same network.
Router interface IP that connects a local network to other networks.
Special address (last in subnet) used to send data to all devices in network.

flashcards.front: What distinguishes network vs host bits?
flashcards.back: Subnet mask determines network bits (1s) and host bits (0s) in IP address.
FE80::/10 range - used for communication within network segment.
Automatic Private IP Addressing (169.254.x.x) when DHCP fails.
